diffCoverage.notes: Focus on listener lifecycle (replace/remove), throttle correctness under back-to-back sends, and lock-hold across await points.

resolvedFindings.resolution: The previous unbounded Vec<Listener> that never stopped sockets is gone. SessionManager::insert gracefully stops a prior listener (Listener::stop, watch-channel based) before replacing it, and remove() stops it on teardown — resolving the explicit-teardown / per-account lifecycle follow-up from listener-events.

findings.evidence: Lifecycle is deterministic and unit-tested with a fake listener: insert_replaces_and_stops_previous_listener proves a re-login stops the old socket exactly once and does not grow the map; remove_stops_listener_and_reports_presence proves teardown + idempotent re-remove. The throttle is computed against an injected `now` Instant so pacing is tested without sleeping (throttle_spaces_same_account_only, throttle_clears_after_interval_elapses). In send_text the throttle lock is released before the await on sleep/send (reserve() returns a Duration and drops its guard), so no Mutex guard is held across an await.

send_text waits the reserved interval with tokio::time::sleep (bounded by the fixed interval, no busy-loop) and delegates the network send to zalo, whose timeouts/retries live in zca-rust. The shared-channel bridge tasks still terminate on receiver/emit error as before.
findings.fix: When a stop/restart command is surfaced to the UI, propagate listener disconnect events so a dropped socket becomes visible; non-blocking follow-up.

summary: Reliability review of the session layer: listener shutdown is now explicit and graceful on both replace and remove (resolving the unbounded-Vec leak), throttle pacing is correct and deterministically tested, and no lock is held across an await on the send path. Live round-trip smoke passed. No blocking findings.
